address: Naresdamri Road, Hua Hin, Prachuap Khiri Khan 77110
best_time_to_visit: Early morning to watch fishing boats unload their catch, or sunset for golden-hour photography

description.en:
## The Heart of Hua Hin's Fishing Heritage

Hua Hin Fishing Pier sits at the northern end of Naresdamri Road and has been the centre of the town's fishing trade for generations. This weathered wooden pier stretches out over the Gulf of Thailand and remains a working dock where colourful squid boats and traditional fishing vessels moor daily. It is one of the most photographed landmarks in Hua Hin and a window into the town's roots as a humble fishing village before it became a royal resort.

## Morning Fish Market

Early risers who arrive around 06:00 to 07:00 are rewarded with the sight of fishing boats returning from their overnight trips. Traders descend on the pier to collect their orders, and you can buy fresh squid, fish, crabs, clams, and mussels directly from the boats at wholesale prices. It is a lively, authentic scene that most tourists miss.

## Sunset and Photography

In the late afternoon the pier transforms into Hua Hin's premier sunset spot. The silhouettes of moored fishing boats against an orange sky create a postcard-perfect scene. Local and visiting photographers line the pier railing around 17:30 to 18:00 to capture the moment.

## Waterfront Seafood Restaurants

Several excellent seafood restaurants are built on their own wooden piers alongside the main dock. Ketsarin, Chaolay, and Jae Kiew are long-established favourites serving ultra-fresh catches at reasonable prices. Dining right over the water with the sound of gentle waves below is a memorable experience.

## Getting There

The pier is at the end of Naresdamri Road, within a 5-minute walk from the Hua Hin Night Market and most central hotels.
description.nl: Historische houten vissteiger in het centrum van de stad, een schilderachtig herkenningspunt en populaire plek voor zonsondergangfoto's

tips: Come at dawn to watch the fishing boats unload — it is the most authentic experience the pier offers
Book a waterfront table at Chaolay or Ketsarin for dinner and combine dining with sunset views
The pier can be slippery from sea spray — wear shoes with grip rather than flip-flops
